sammwyy/MikuMikuBeam ·GitHub ⭐ 5657 Go · Streaming, Live-Video, RTMP, HLS, VTuber, Cpp

What it is

A lightweight and low-latency live streaming server supporting RTMP, HLS, and WebRTC, optimized for anime and V-Tuber community needs.

Problems solved

  • Complexity of configuring OBS to private servers; high latency in traditional HLS streaming; resource-heavy nature of standard streaming servers like Nginx-RTMP.

How to get started

Download the binary; configure servers.json; start with './mikubeam'; point your encoder (OBS) to the RTMP endpoint; access the web player on the provided port.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

sammwyy/MikuMikuBeam is positioned as a a lightweight and low-latency live streaming server supporting rtmp, hls, and webrtc, optimized for anime and v-tuber community needs. It aims to provide developers with a robust, Go-based solution for an open-source network stresser tool but it's hatsune miku.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Complexity of configuring OBS to private servers; high latency in traditional HLS streaming; resource-heavy nature of standard streaming servers like Nginx-RTMP.. sammwyy/MikuMikuBeam addresses this by a lightweight and low-latency live streaming server supporting rtmp, hls, and webrtc, optimized for anime and v-tuber community needs, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using Go, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around an open-source network stresser tool but it's hatsune miku. The implementation utilizes Go's strengths to provide a lightweight and low-latency live streaming server supporting rtmp, hls, and webrtc, optimized for anime and v-tuber community needs, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, download the binary; configure servers.json; start with './mikubeam'; point your encoder (obs) to the rtmp endpoint; access the web player on the provided port. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, Go-powered utility that streamlines an open-source network stresser tool but it's hatsune miku; it effectively tackles Complexity of configuring OBS to private servers; high latency in traditional HLS streaming; resource-heavy nature of standard streaming servers like Nginx-RTMP. while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
